blocks.html_en:
I think the Kimi thing is super cool, especially that they have so many RNN/linear attention layers (3x more than they have full attention). I haven't yet tried it though. It seems like it would be extremely reasonable for long context tasks and I guess this fits the times.

I suspect that the reason it has so many parameters is the same reason that compute optimal xLSTMs have some many parameters, and the success of this model makes me a bit unhappy that we haven't gotten an xLSTM-style model of huge size developed in Europe.

Obviously these guys are very pragmatic, they're probably not committed to anything other than what works on their internal evaluations, so they still have ordinary attention layers in the model and so on, and one can't be guaranteed that the people who come up with a good model then do the engineering in an ideal way, but I still think the success of Kimi shows what could have been if we had enough big supercomputers for LLM training and made them available to the right people-- because this is basically Hochreiter's thing. It's RNNs, or well, mostly RNNs.

sentences.en: I think the Kimi thing is super cool, especially that they have so many RNN/linear attention layers (3x more than they have full attention).
sentences.zh: 我认为 Kimi 的东西非常酷，特别是他们有这么多的 RNN/线性注意力层（比他们的完全注意力多 3 倍）。

sentences.en: I haven't yet tried it though.
sentences.zh: 不过我还没有尝试过。

sentences.en: It seems like it would be extremely reasonable for long context tasks and I guess this fits the times.I suspect that the reason it has so many parameters is the same reason that compute optimal xLSTMs have some many parameters, and the success of this model makes me a bit unhappy that we haven't gotten an xLSTM-style model of huge size developed in Europe.Obviously these guys are very pragmatic, they're probably not committed to anything other than what works on their internal evaluations, so they still have ordinary attention layers in the model and so on, and one can't be guaranteed that the people who come up with a good model then do the engineering in an ideal way, but I still think the success of Kimi shows what could have been if we had enough big supercomputers for LLM training and made them available to the right people-- because this is basically Hochreiter's thing.
sentences.zh: 对于长上下文任务来说，这似乎是非常合理的，我想这符合时代。我怀疑它有这么多参数的原因与计算最优 xLSTM 有很多参数的原因是一样的，这个模型的成功让我有点不高兴，因为我们还没有在欧洲开发出一个大规模的 xLSTM 风格的模型。显然这些人非常务实，除了内部评估之外，他们可能不会致力于任何其他事情，所以他们仍然有普通的注意力层模型等等，我们不能保证提出好的模型的人会以理想的方式进行工程，但我仍然认为 Kimi 的成功表明，如果我们有足够的大型超级计算机用于法学硕士培训，并将它们提供给合适的人，会发生什么——因为这基本上是 Hochreiter 的事情。

sentences.en: It's RNNs, or well, mostly RNNs.
sentences.zh: 它是 RNN，或者说，主要是 RNN。

blocks.html_en:
I think the Kimi thing is super cool, especially that they have so many RNN/linear attention layers (3x more than they have full attention). I haven't yet tried it though. It seems like it would be extremely reasonable for long context tasks and I guess this fits the times.

I suspect that the reason it has so many parameters is the same reason that compute optimal xLSTMs have some many parameters, and the success of this model makes me a bit unhappy that we haven't gotten an xLSTM-style model of huge size developed in Europe.

Obviously these guys are very pragmatic, they're probably not committed to anything other than what works on their internal evaluations, so they still have ordinary attention layers in the model and so on, and one can't be guaranteed that the people who come up with a good model then do the engineering in an ideal way, but I still think the success of Kimi shows what could have been if we had enough big supercomputers for LLM training and made them available to the right people-- because this is basically Hochreiter's thing. It's RNNs, or well, mostly RNNs.

sentences.en: I haven't yet tried it though.
sentences.zh: 不过我还没有尝试过。

blocks.html_en: Wasn't one of the original intents of the transformer architecture to get rid of RNN because they cannot be parallelized? Not an expert, just read a few papers several years ago.

sentences.en: Wasn't one of the original intents of the transformer architecture to get rid of RNN because they cannot be parallelized?
sentences.zh: Transformer 架构的初衷之一不就是摆脱 RNN，因为它们无法并行化吗？

sentences.en: Not an expert, just read a few papers several years ago.
sentences.zh: 不是专家，几年前只读过几篇论文。

blocks.html_en:
Yes, although then people came up with reasonable ways of parallelizing RNNs entirely or in part anyway. There's also the need for lookups in transformers. Queries still need to be multiplied with old keys, all of them, so RNNs don't have to be totally incompatible with parallelism. It's just that you can't have a matrix inside them like in the linear RNN h_t = Ah_t + Bx_t that you have to multiply together step after step. If A is an input-dependent scalar or something you can multiply together easily you're fine.

mLSTM layers are completely parallel (the update rule for the cell state is C_t = f_t C_{t-1} + i_t x_t where f_t and i_t are gates that can be computed from x_t alone, x_t is the input at time t and this means that you can compute F_s = \prod_{s<t} f_s as fast as a cumsum and an exponential and then compute C_t = \sum_{s<t} F_s i_t x_t, again as a cumsum, so it's as good as if though it were parallel). I think the sLSTM layers that are the other component of the xLSTM have something else like this and presumably there's some trick also to training the Kimi "delta attention" RNN.

I'm not sure whether this is hardware or optimization dependent to some degree, but I get the impression that good custom kernels are an important part of this kind of thing.

sentences.en: Yes, although then people came up with reasonable ways of parallelizing RNNs entirely or in part anyway.
sentences.zh: 是的，尽管后来人们想出了完全或部分并行化 RNN 的合理方法。

sentences.en: There's also the need for lookups in transformers.
sentences.zh: 还需要在变压器中进行查找。

sentences.en: Queries still need to be multiplied with old keys, all of them, so RNNs don't have to be totally incompatible with parallelism.
sentences.zh: 查询仍然需要与所有旧密钥相乘，因此 RNN 不必与并行性完全不兼容。

sentences.en: It's just that you can't have a matrix inside them like in the linear RNN h_t = Ah_t + Bx_t that you have to multiply together step after step.
sentences.zh: 只是你不能像线性 RNN h_t = Ah_t + Bx_t 那样在它们内部有一个矩阵，你必须一步一步相乘。

sentences.en: If A is an input-dependent scalar or something you can multiply together easily you're fine.mLSTM layers are completely parallel (the update rule for the cell state is C_t = f_t C_{t-1} + i_t x_t where f_t and i_t are gates that can be computed from x_t alone, x_t is the input at time t and this means that you can compute F_s = \prod_{s<t} f_s as fast as a cumsum and an exponential and then compute C_t = \sum_{s<t} F_s i_t x_t, again as a cumsum, so it's as good as if though it were parallel).
sentences.zh: 如果 A 是一个依赖于输入的标量或者可以轻松相乘的东西，那就没问题了。mLSTM 层是完全并行的（单元状态的更新规则是 C_t = f_t C_{t-1} + i_t x_t，其中 f_t 和 i_t 是可以单独从 x_t 计算的门，x_t 是时间 t 的输入，这意味着您可以像 a 一样快地计算 F_s = \prod_{s<t} f_s cumsum 和一个指数，然后计算 C_t = \sum_{s<t} F_s i_t x_t，再次作为 cumsum，所以它就像并行一样好）。

sentences.en: I think the sLSTM layers that are the other component of the xLSTM have something else like this and presumably there's some trick also to training the Kimi "delta attention" RNN.I'm not sure whether this is hardware or optimization dependent to some degree, but I get the impression that good custom kernels are an important part of this kind of thing.
sentences.zh: 我认为 sLSTM 层是 xLSTM 的另一个组件，也有类似的东西，并且可能还有一些技巧可以训练 Kimi“增量注意力”RNN。我不确定这是否在某种程度上依赖于硬件或优化，但我的印象是良好的自定义内核是此类事情的重要组成部分。

blocks.html_en:
Yes, but how would it have been if the EU governments just piled on, subsidized whoever had reasonable qualifications who wanted to try, straight-up funded accelerator development so that the chips don't just become investment in US AI companies, straight up built the supercomputers for training, straight up offered access to groups that had previously had reasonable success in training some model.

I don't think it would even be that expensive. I think with EU chips we could do it for 20 billion EUR + 5 billion EUR per year. After all, an H200 doesn't cost 40k to manufacture, probably one-tenth, and if we are making the chips we can get the money circulating just as the Americans have.

Add in some state effort to create suitable training data-- maybe encourage academics to create some things that are AI-friendly, and let all the people trying have that. There would hardly be anyone in the game other than us if we went for this package.

We already have VSORA. Maybe Euclyd is ready soon too. If enough are ordered we have 85% of the inference need gone, so that whatever accelerators are freed can be repurposed for supercomputing and training? Maybe we could have some organized effort to make many of the VSORA and Euclyd chips so that the chips we need for training are freed up while we wait for our own training chips.

Edit: So in the end I think the thing we need to do is to order enough VSORA Jotunn8 cards and enough Euclyd cards that we flood the inference market so much that the price of H200eds etc. drops and we can buy them second hand and assemble huge supercomputers out of them. This may require some diplomatic effort from the commission, something like 20B EUR almost immediately and some coordination, but it's easy, it's doable and it requires only being a little bit decisive. Since these cards are faster less memory will be tied up in serving the models, so it'd have a positive effect on memory prices too, which in turn would have a positive effect on European supercomputer centers, who are currently of course constrained by high memory prices.

sentences.en: Yes, but how would it have been if the EU governments just piled on, subsidized whoever had reasonable qualifications who wanted to try, straight-up funded accelerator development so that the chips don't just become investment in US AI companies, straight up built the supercomputers for training, straight up offered access to groups that had previously had reasonable success in training some model.I don't think it would even be that expensive.
sentences.zh: 是的，但如果欧盟政府只是大量补贴那些有合理资格想要尝试的人，直接资助加速器开发，这样芯片就不仅仅成为对美国人工智能公司的投资，直接建造用于培训的超级计算机，直接向以前在训练某些模型方面取得相当成功的团体提供机会。我认为它甚至不会那么昂贵。

sentences.en: I think with EU chips we could do it for 20 billion EUR + 5 billion EUR per year.
sentences.zh: 我认为利用欧盟芯片，我们每年可以花费 200 亿欧元 + 50 亿欧元来实现这一目标。

sentences.en: After all, an H200 doesn't cost 40k to manufacture, probably one-tenth, and if we are making the chips we can get the money circulating just as the Americans have.Add in some state effort to create suitable training data-- maybe encourage academics to create some things that are AI-friendly, and let all the people trying have that.
sentences.zh: 毕竟，H200 的制造成本不会是 4 万，可能是十分之一，如果我们制造芯片，我们就可以像美国人一样让资金流通。再加上一些国家努力来创建合适的训练数据——也许会鼓励学者创建一些对人工智能友好的东西，让所有尝试的人都拥有它。

sentences.en: There would hardly be anyone in the game other than us if we went for this package.We already have VSORA.
sentences.zh: 如果我们选择这个包，除了我们之外，游戏中几乎没有其他人了。我们已经有了 VSORA。

sentences.en: Maybe Euclyd is ready soon too.
sentences.zh: 也许Euclyd也很快就准备好了。

sentences.en: If enough are ordered we have 85% of the inference need gone, so that whatever accelerators are freed can be repurposed for supercomputing and training?
sentences.zh: 如果订购了足够的数量，我们就可以消除 85% 的推理需求，这样释放的任何加速器都可以重新用于超级计算和训练？

sentences.en: Maybe we could have some organized effort to make many of the VSORA and Euclyd chips so that the chips we need for training are freed up while we wait for our own training chips.Edit: So in the end I think the thing we need to do is to order enough VSORA Jotunn8 cards and enough Euclyd cards that we flood the inference market so much that the price of H200eds etc. drops and we can buy them second hand and assemble huge supercomputers out of them.
sentences.zh: 也许我们可以做一些有组织的努力来制造许多VSORA和Euclyd芯片，以便在我们等待自己的训练芯片时释放我们训练所需的芯片。编辑：所以最后我认为我们需要做的就是订购足够的VSORA Jotunn8卡和足够的Euclyd卡，这样我们就能大量涌入推理市场，以至于H200eds等的价格下降，我们可以购买二手的并用它们组装巨大的超级计算机。

sentences.en: This may require some diplomatic effort from the commission, something like 20B EUR almost immediately and some coordination, but it's easy, it's doable and it requires only being a little bit decisive.
sentences.zh: 这可能需要委员会做出一些外交努力，比如几乎立即提供 20B 欧元和一些协调，但这很容易，是可行的，只需要一点果断。

sentences.en: Since these cards are faster less memory will be tied up in serving the models, so it'd have a positive effect on memory prices too, which in turn would have a positive effect on European supercomputer centers, who are currently of course constrained by high memory prices.
sentences.zh: 由于这些卡速度更快，服务模型所需的内存更少，因此它也会对内存价格产生积极影响，这反过来又会对欧洲超级计算机中心产生积极影响，而欧洲超级计算机中心目前当然受到高内存价格的限制。

blocks.html_en:
Yes, but how would it have been if the EU governments just piled on, subsidized whoever had reasonable qualifications who wanted to try, straight-up funded accelerator development so that the chips don't just become investment in US AI companies, straight up built the supercomputers for training, straight up offered access to groups that had previously had reasonable success in training some model.

I don't think it would even be that expensive. I think with EU chips we could do it for 20 billion EUR + 5 billion EUR per year. After all, an H200 doesn't cost 40k to manufacture, probably one-tenth, and if we are making the chips we can get the money circulating just as the Americans have.

Add in some state effort to create suitable training data-- maybe encourage academics to create some things that are AI-friendly, and let all the people trying have that. There would hardly be anyone in the game other than us if we went for this package.

We already have VSORA. Maybe Euclyd is ready soon too. If enough are ordered we have 85% of the inference need gone, so that whatever accelerators are freed can be repurposed for supercomputing and training? Maybe we could have some organized effort to make many of the VSORA and Euclyd chips so that the chips we need for training are freed up while we wait for our own training chips.

Edit: So in the end I think the thing we need to do is to order enough VSORA Jotunn8 cards and enough Euclyd cards that we flood the inference market so much that the price of H200eds etc. drops and we can buy them second hand and assemble huge supercomputers out of them. This may require some diplomatic effort from the commission, something like 20B EUR almost immediately and some coordination, but it's easy, it's doable and it requires only being a little bit decisive. Since these cards are faster less memory will be tied up in serving the models, so it'd have a positive effect on memory prices too, which in turn would have a positive effect on European supercomputer centers, who are currently of course constrained by high memory prices.

sentences.en: Yes, but how would it have been if the EU governments just piled on, subsidized whoever had reasonable qualifications who wanted to try, straight-up funded accelerator development so that the chips don't just become investment in US AI companies, straight up built the supercomputers for training, straight up offered access to groups that had previously had reasonable success in training some model.I don't think it would even be that expensive.
sentences.zh: 是的，但如果欧盟政府只是大量补贴那些有合理资格想要尝试的人，直接资助加速器开发，这样芯片就不仅仅成为对美国人工智能公司的投资，直接建造用于培训的超级计算机，直接向以前在训练某些模型方面取得相当成功的团体提供机会。我认为它甚至不会那么昂贵。

sentences.zh: 我认为利用欧盟芯片，我们每年可以花费 200 亿欧元 + 50 亿欧元来实现这一目标。

blocks.html_en:
I just pay metered usage via OpenRouter. I've spent no more than $10-$20/mo, but my usage is, by the standards of so-called "agentic coding" these days, very light and confined.

I doubt my workflows match the profile of "daily driver" as you're conceiving of it. I'm mostly in the IDE chat box asking for a hand here and there, but no "long horizon" / big vision / architecture things. Occasionally, I'll solicit a general opinion, quite abstractly, which isn't very token-intensive. My contexts are tiny and I aim to keep them that way.

The only token-intensive thing I do is occasional analyses of open-source codebases along the lines of "what is the actual behaviour when message X comes in and is malformed like this?", or something else similarly far-reaching. For that, I use DeepSeek's Reasonix, which they savvily dropped at the exact moment the sting of Claude API pricing became a sensation. It's a very cache-oriented harness, and delivers on its promise of being very economical. I doubt I've spent more than $10 with DeepSeek over the months I've used it. When I want a harness to inhale a codebase and give me an answer, Reasonix has been the go-to for a while now, and it's great. DeepSeek-V4-Flash is enough for many simple things, though I just run it on DeepSeek-V4-Pro because it's so cheap that I don't care.

I doubt my workflows match the profile of "daily driver" as you're conceiving of it. I'm mostly in the IDE chat box asking for a hand here and there, but no "long horizon" / big vision / architecture things. blocks.html_en:
A breath of fresh air to pause new subscriptions rather than the Google approach of quietly nerfing the limits and hoping you don't notice you're getting less value for your monthly/annual subscription.

Limits may change without notice, including due to capacity constraints. When there’s a large increase in activity in Gemini Apps, we may change limits to maintain a high standard of quality. [1]

[1]: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16275805

blocks.html_en:
In our multi-agent game coding evaluations, we usually see Chinese models struggle in one-shot reasoning but make up for it with tool use and iterating towards better solutions. Kimi K3 follows that pattern, ranking 19th in one-shot coding and 3rd in agentic coding (where the model gets a harness and tools and many calls to iterate toward better code). Only Sol and Fable have better average agentic coding submissions.

Agentic coding is what's most relevant to software engineers, but so is speed, which is a real usability issue right now. Third-party inference providers like Fireworks have bridged the gap for some previous releases.

It's exciting to see the open weights frontier becoming the norm. Competing on having the frontier model is going to be an increasingly difficult business.

Data at https://gertlabs.com/rankings?mode=agentic_coding

blocks.html_en: Ive been pleasantly surprised with the quality of this model. Its really good at code review and PR review. But right now due to them being so overloaded + this being a big model, its SO slow. It takes forever to do a relatively simple code review.

blocks.html_en:
Oh, okay. Didn't know that, as I haven't tried K3 myself -- I'm quite content with Kimi K2.7-Code for the moment, which OpenRouter serves me to via US-based inference providers. Supposedly.

Obviously, not relevant if your reason for being here is to ferret out the excitement about K3 specifically. But if this is your first time trying Kimi, try K2.6 or K2.7! If my experience is any indication, you'll be blown away by those, too.

blocks.html_en:
Curious if this kimi moment is net growth (jervons paradox - abundance = more consumption) or just people shifting to cheaper model

Is there a good source for total token consumption between the various labs / openrouter / etc?

blocks.html_en: If this was about shifting to cheaper models I think people would be going to DeepSeek for the v4 Flash. Kimi's latest is more expensive than most other Chinese models so I'd say this is more a wagonist "hey this new model really rocks; y'all gotta check it out!". Question is how much it'll stand up under the scrutiny. And those US labs are probably getting pretty nervous.

sentences.zh: 问题是它能在多大程度上经得起审查。

sentences.zh: 那些美国实验室可能会变得非常紧张。

blocks.html_en: I wonder if anthropic and openai will remain relevant simply due to the fact that they're the only ones that are able to handle this much demand for the forseeable future? My bet would be that companies would probably not be too happy with employee time being wasted on outages and other related issues when it already costs so much.

sentences.en: I wonder if anthropic and openai will remain relevant simply due to the fact that they're the only ones that are able to handle this much demand for the forseeable future?
sentences.zh: 我想知道 anthropic 和 openai 是否会仅仅因为它们是唯一能够在可预见的未来满足如此大的需求而保持相关性？

sentences.en: My bet would be that companies would probably not be too happy with employee time being wasted on outages and other related issues when it already costs so much.
sentences.zh: 我敢打赌，公司可能不会太高兴员工的时间被浪费在停电和其他相关问题上，因为成本已经如此之高。

blocks.html_en: Yes, this is the story I see playing out at my employer. Software is cheap, but the know-how to host and be accountable for uptime still keeps the actual deployments consolidated among a few groups. But yes, there are a bunch of productivity helpers that people have vibe coded that live on their laptops.

sentences.en: Yes, this is the story I see playing out at my employer.
sentences.zh: 是的，这就是我在我的雇主身上看到的故事。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic also had a demand problem just a couple months ago, which led them to this on- vs off-peak usage thing that was driving people crazy, along with frequent outages. Then they made a deal with xAI and all has been mostly well since. Moonshot is making deals right now as well for compute.

sentences.en: Anthropic also had a demand problem just a couple months ago, which led them to this on- vs off-peak usage thing that was driving people crazy, along with frequent outages.
sentences.zh: 就在几个月前，Anthropic 还遇到了需求问题，这导致他们出现了高峰使用和非高峰使用的问题，这让人们抓狂，并且经常出现断电。

sentences.en: Then they made a deal with xAI and all has been mostly well since.
sentences.zh: 然后他们与 xAI 达成了协议，此后一切都进展顺利。

sentences.en: Moonshot is making deals right now as well for compute.
sentences.zh: Moonshot 目前也在计算方面达成交易。

blocks.html_en: A lot of OpenAI/Anthropic compute actually belongs to the hyperscalers. They have long term contracts and will be able to charge some kind of margin for access to the compute they've secured. However I don't think this would last long as the hyperscalers will be able to undercut them.

sentences.en: A lot of OpenAI/Anthropic compute actually belongs to the hyperscalers.
sentences.zh: 许多 OpenAI/Anthropic 计算实际上属于超大规模者。

sentences.en: They have long term contracts and will be able to charge some kind of margin for access to the compute they've secured.
sentences.zh: 他们签订了长期合同，并且能够为访问他们所获得的计算资源收取某种保证金。

sentences.en: However I don't think this would last long as the hyperscalers will be able to undercut them.
sentences.zh: 然而，我认为这种情况不会持续太久，因为超大规模企业将能够削弱它们。

blocks.html_en: I expect what's most likely to happen is that either Anthropic or OpenAI end up becoming a vendor of record for the government and get a bailout. And then their whole business model will be serving use niches which would be considered too sensitive for Chinese models. That's the only plausible business model I can see here.

blocks.html_en:
Damn some timing, I just signed up few hours ago and within 3-4 hours spent over 50 bucks on my personal project (didn't realize that the launcher script I copied from an example had set effort to max). This is API use that consumes from your recharge amount. It found tons of issues with the code generated by Sonnet 4.6 (yeah I know, older model). Also didn't seem to burn through context window that fast (1M). The generated code works but quality could be better. Works fine with Claude code cli. The standout aspect was strong analytical abilities and a much more sobering and cautious tone (which is a good thing).

Will have to watch cost carefully though. If only I could get it at DeepSeek adjacent prices.

sentences.en: Damn some timing, I just signed up few hours ago and within 3-4 hours spent over 50 bucks on my personal project (didn't realize that the launcher script I copied from an example had set effort to max).
sentences.zh: 该死的时机，我几个小时前刚刚注册，在 3-4 小时内就在我的个人项目上花费了 50 多美元（没有意识到我从示例复制的启动器脚本已将工作量设置为最大）。

sentences.en: This is API use that consumes from your recharge amount.
sentences.zh: 这是 API 使用，会消耗您的充值金额。

sentences.zh: 它发现 Sonnet 4.6（是的，我知道，旧型号）生成的代码存在大量问题。

sentences.en: Also didn't seem to burn through context window that fast (1M).
sentences.zh: 似乎也没有那么快地烧毁上下文窗口（1M）。

sentences.en: The generated code works but quality could be better.
sentences.zh: 生成的代码可以工作，但质量可以更好。

sentences.en: Works fine with Claude code cli.
sentences.zh: 与 Claude 代码 cli 配合良好。

sentences.en: The standout aspect was strong analytical abilities and a much more sobering and cautious tone (which is a good thing).Will have to watch cost carefully though.
sentences.zh: 突出的方面是强大的分析能力和更加清醒和谨慎的语气（这是一件好事）。不过必须仔细观察成本。

sentences.en: If only I could get it at DeepSeek adjacent prices.
sentences.zh: 如果我能以 DeepSeek 相邻价格买到就好了。

blocks.html_en: Early in the morning on the day Kimi K3 was released, I tried using Multi-Agent to execute a relatively complex research report. The whole process took five hours, involved 12 roles, and ultimately produced a 54,000-word report. I read through it carefully, and my feeling is that it could directly serve as an outline for a book or a documentary. I also observed the writing process—especially the repeated verification of potentially inaccurate data sources, as well as the reminders and adjustment suggestions regarding certain platforms. In short, I am very satisfied with this report, and I will use it as a reference for the subsequent documentary.

sentences.en: Early in the morning on the day Kimi K3 was released, I tried using Multi-Agent to execute a relatively complex research report.
sentences.zh: Kimi K3发布当天一大早，我尝试使用Multi-Agent执行一份相对复杂的研究报告。

sentences.en: The whole process took five hours, involved 12 roles, and ultimately produced a 54,000-word report.
sentences.zh: 整个过程耗时5个小时，涉及12个角色，最终产出了一份54000字的报告。

sentences.en: I read through it carefully, and my feeling is that it could directly serve as an outline for a book or a documentary.
sentences.zh: 我仔细读了一遍，感觉可以直接作为一本书或一部纪录片的提纲。

sentences.en: I also observed the writing process—especially the repeated verification of potentially inaccurate data sources, as well as the reminders and adjustment suggestions regarding certain platforms.
sentences.zh: 我也观察了写作过程，特别是对可能不准确的数据来源的反复验证，以及对某些平台的提醒和调整建议。

sentences.en: In short, I am very satisfied with this report, and I will use it as a reference for the subsequent documentary.
sentences.zh: 总之，我对这份报告非常满意，并将作为后续纪录片的参考。

sentences.en: I also observed the writing process—especially the repeated verification of potentially inaccurate data sources, as well as the reminders and adjustment suggestions regarding certain platforms.
sentences.zh: 我也观察了写作过程，特别是对可能不准确的数据来源的反复验证，以及对某些平台的提醒和调整建议。

sentences.en: In short, I am very satisfied with this report, and I will use it as a reference for the subsequent documentary.
sentences.zh: 总之，我对这份报告非常满意，并将作为后续纪录片的参考。

blocks.html_en:
I tried it through siliconflow (I have no relationship to them other than as a normal customer) with the command line Pi agent and it's pretty good at adding features to a rust lang code repo. Compared to Deepseek V4 Pro there, M3 is about 3X the cost for non-cached input tokens and 5X the cost for output tokens. (both offering a 1M token context window). It certainly wasn't worh 5X the output of the Deepseek model for my uses, though.

I couldn't find any info on how they (siliconflow) quantized the model. I think some of the other ineference service aggregators might offer it as well, already.

blocks.html_en:
Currently using their 100 USD subscription, considering going up to their 200 USD subscription, because the model is approximately as capable as Opus (maybe sometimes better, sometimes worse) but less annoying to use in practice, a bit less of slop phrasing: https://blog.kronis.dev/blog/ai-slop-is-a-self-inflicted-tra...

The token limits do feel a bit less than I get with Anthropic Max 5x, but maybe that's because I've mostly been running it on Max reasoning (oh and Anthropic is also temporarily boosting the limits, who knows, it's hard to keep track of all of this stuff exactly) and there's plenty of tasks where High is still close enough in performance. The token limits still feel a bit more generous proportionally to the price compared to what I got when trying out the 65 USD tier of GLM Coding Subscription with GLM 5.2, and that was with the ZCode usage discount as well, though I did enjoy that harness.

Plus, if I decide to go with Kimi's annual pricing, then it'd come out to only around 159 USD per month or 139 EUR per month, which is really good and pretty close to what I pay Anthropic anyways: https://www.kimi.com/help/membership/membership-pricing

sentences.en: Currently using their 100 USD subscription, considering going up to their 200 USD subscription, because the model is approximately as capable as Opus (maybe sometimes better, sometimes worse) but less annoying to use in practice, a bit less of slop phrasing: https://blog.kronis.dev/blog/ai-slop-is-a-self-inflicted-tra...The token limits do feel a bit less than I get with Anthropic Max 5x, but maybe that's because I've mostly been running it on Max reasoning (oh and Anthropic is also temporarily boosting the limits, who knows, it's hard to keep track of all of this stuff exactly) and there's plenty of tasks where High is still close enough in performance.
sentences.zh: 目前正在使用他们的 100 美元订阅，考虑升级到 200 美元订阅，因为该模型的功能大约与 Opus 一样（也许有时更好，有时更差），但在实践中使用起来不那么烦人，少一点草率措辞：https://blog.kronis.dev/blog/ai-slop-is-a-self-inflicted-tra...代币限制确实感觉比我使用 Anthropic Max 5x 少一点，但也许这是因为我主要是在 Max 推理上运行它（哦，Anthropic 也暂时提高了限制，谁知道呢，很难准确地跟踪所有这些东西）并且有很多任务的 High 性能仍然足够接近。

sentences.en: The token limits still feel a bit more generous proportionally to the price compared to what I got when trying out the 65 USD tier of GLM Coding Subscription with GLM 5.2, and that was with the ZCode usage discount as well, though I did enjoy that harness.Plus, if I decide to go with Kimi's annual pricing, then it'd come out to only around 159 USD per month or 139 EUR per month, which is really good and pretty close to what I pay Anthropic anyways: https://www.kimi.com/help/membership/membership-pricing
sentences.zh: 与我在使用 GLM 5.2 尝试 65 美元级别的 GLM 编码订阅时获得的价格相比，代币限制仍然感觉更慷慨一些，而且这还包括 ZCode 使用折扣，尽管我确实喜欢这种工具。另外，如果我决定采用 Kimi 的年度定价，那么每月只需大约 159 美元或 139 欧元，这非常好，非常接近我所支付的价格无论如何都是人为的：https://www.kimi.com/help/membership/membership-pricing

blocks.html_en: another reason why it’s great to have open weights: inference providers and your own hardware can ensure that you get the tokens you need even if a lab itself hits roadblocks (and there have been many in quality and availability)

sentences.en: another reason why it’s great to have open weights: inference providers and your own hardware can ensure that you get the tokens you need even if a lab itself hits roadblocks (and there have been many in quality and availability)
sentences.zh: 拥有开放权重的另一个原因是：推理提供商和您自己的硬件可以确保您获得所需的代币，即使实验室本身遇到障碍（质量和可用性方面存在许多障碍）

blocks.html_en: They could've simply cut the usage limits, removed K3 from the plan, added a weekly cap, and called it "fair usage." Anthropic has been running that playbook for a while now, and they're a trillion-dollar company.

sentences.en: They could've simply cut the usage limits, removed K3 from the plan, added a weekly cap, and called it "fair usage."
sentences.zh: 他们可以简单地削减使用限制，将 K3 从计划中删除，添加每周上限，并称之为“公平使用”。

sentences.en: Anthropic has been running that playbook for a while now, and they're a trillion-dollar company.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 已经执行这个策略有一段时间了，他们是一家价值数万亿美元的公司。

blocks.html_en:
To be honest, they have both 5 hour caps, weekly caps and also monthly caps:

  Total usage
  12.85%
  Resets in 2026-08-17
  
  5-hour usage
  71.97%
  Resets in 07-20 03:14
  
  7-day usage
  31.29%
  Resets in 07-24 10:14

Still, I can respect the commitment to not over provisioning.

blocks.html_en:
The rate limits on the $20 plan go so fast that it's easy to use my weekly budget in only a couple days. I can't imagine also trying to use K3 on the plan.

Back in January with K2.5 it felt like I could use it a lot more.

sentences.en: The rate limits on the $20 plan go so fast that it's easy to use my weekly budget in only a couple days.
sentences.zh: 20 美元计划的费率限制变化得非常快，以至于我很容易在几天内就用完我的每周预算。

sentences.en: I can't imagine also trying to use K3 on the plan.Back in January with K2.5 it felt like I could use it a lot more.
sentences.zh: 我无法想象在计划中也尝试使用 K3。早在一月份使用 K2.5 时，感觉我可以更多地使用它。

blocks.html_en:
unfortunately that probably won't be solved anytime soon because the entire point is to make you blow through it all asap and buy more.

there's always a push to make the models smarter but everyone was (and still is) convinced that if we just throw more hardware at the problem and then just tell the ai to re-read its entire ''thought'' process several dozen times it'll somehow all work out and... yeah.

and it kinda works but they'll guzzle your funds very quickly now.

unless serious strides are made in efficency this problem's only gonna get worse and worse.

content_html_zh: > 在过去 48 小时内，需求已接近我们当前容量的极限。 为了保护现有订阅者的体验，我们暂时暂停新订阅并优先考虑当前会员的计算。 现有订阅用户不受影响。这是一个值得一读的美丽段落，一家优先考虑当前客户并专注于让他们满意而不是仅仅关注快速增长的公司。我记得当 GitHub 这样做时，人们非常沮丧。 无论是当时还是现在，这都是正确的举动！Github 上的人们并没有因为暂停新注册而感到不安。 现有客户对基于代币的计费方式取代了他们每月 40 美元的无限制订阅感到不满。人们对 Github 暂停新注册感到非常不满。 基于代币的计费是另一回事。> 一家优先考虑当前客户并专注于让他们满意而不是仅仅关注快速增长的公司。我一直讨厌每个网站上的“登录”按钮都比“注册”按钮小。> 我一直讨厌每个网站上的“登录”按钮都比“注册”按钮小。我一直认为这是因为它是最不常见的流程。 一旦你注册了，你就登录了，所以你唯一需要“登录”按钮的时候是在一台新计算机上，或者在注销切换帐户之后，或者该服务提供了“记住我”复选框（不要这样做）并且默认情况下没有选中它（绝对不要这样做），或者如果用户真的不想被记住（例如，他们使用了隐私浏览）。如果您必须在任何帐户上使用“登录”定期进行，总体流程中的某些内容需要改进。> 您唯一需要使用“登录”按钮的时间是在新计算机上，或者在注销以切换帐户之后。对于每个客户来说，这仍然比注册更频繁。只有当优先考虑增长而不是长期客户时，它才有意义。我注册的每个网站都至少登录过一次。 通常不止一次。 频率不是原因，而是针对注册进行优化。 一旦你让某人注册，即使按钮有点小，他们也会登录。您注册一次并登录任意次数。 不知道注册是如何变得越来越频繁的。 我一生中可能最多使用过两次的东西。 我什至无法开始计算登录次数。您的令牌应该有您需要再次登录的范围内的到期时间。我更喜欢 999 年的登录 cookie。设计良好的浏览器存储 cookie 的安全性与内置密码管理器类似事实并非如此，您需要提供密码才能读取浏览器中保存的密码，而开发人员工具可以轻松读取 cookie 也可以通过编程方式查询 Cookie，而密码需要让用户自动填充活动并与输入元素交互大多数扩展程序实际上可以完全访问您的 cookie，而不是您的密码 - 这是真正的攻击媒介（会话渗透），被 had acteues 积极使用有 ofc 服务器设置的 cookie 不是以编程方式可查询，但这不是常态（并且它们仍然通过开发人员工具可见，因此核心声明仍然不真实）> Cookie 也可以通过编程方式查询，误导性陈述，本质上是不真实的。> 大多数扩展程序都可以有效地完全访问您的 Cookie。False。> 有 ofc 服务器设置的 Cookie 不能以编程方式查询，但这不是规范，这是登录 Cookie 的规范。> 并且它们仍然可以通过开发人员工具看到，这与参数无关。我非常确定服务器设置的 cookie 是身份验证的标准，至少在服务器端设置它们时是这样。似乎是一个不同的问题。 您无法以无法购买新硬件的价格提供硬件，因此没有太多选择。 但仅仅为了降低需求而对现有客户定价是一个大胆的举动，特别是如果你可以简单地解决供应问题的话。如果说有什么不同的话，那就是这表明 Kimi K3 的价格被低估，从而迫使计算资源进行配给。 Anthropic 甚至在 $50/mtok 点上努力避免 503 错误。我会冷静下来；风险投资公司正在向人工智能注入如此多的资金，这可能只是 OpenAI/Anthropic 将资金注入代币燃烧机，以尝试提取他们的数据集（当然是转表）。个人轶事：昨天我用尽了 Claude 的用法，所以我决定花 20 美元尝试一下 Kimi。 登录、付费、下载 Kimi Code，将其设置为使用 K3 并提示如下内容：“检查此存储库并找到可以作为与硬件、I/O、线程控制或网络相关的输入传递的所有设置。 生成一份报告”。它思考了大约 12 分钟，然后告诉我我已经用完了每日配额。 （第二天《神鬼寓言》在 3m 中完成了相同的任务）如果您想购买 K3 计划，请不要购买 20 美元的计划。我觉得这是正确的做法。 特别是如果您使用自己的积分。 除非这是一项令人讨厌的长任务。通常它会在即将完成时被中断。所以我可能来自一个完全不同的世界，因为我使用完全自托管的模型 - 但是在您的工作流程中等待 12 分钟而没有任何反馈真的很常见吗？ 我不断地观察我的模型并跟随它所走的路径，如果它走上了一条死路，我就会切断它或引导它。 我只是好奇 12 分钟没有反馈的情况是否真的很常见？好吧，需要明确的是，有一些反馈（例如，它正在调用子代理并打印一些思考消息）。也就是说，是的，对我来说，等待 10、20、有时甚至 30 分钟而不操纵模型或查看其正在做什么是很常见的。 我通常在开始时写一个非常详细的提示，描述问题、用例、要做的测试以及成功的定义；然后我有时会要求它起草一个计划并阅读一下，但之后我只需按 Enter 键，让它运行并在完成后返回。最后，我对代码和功能进行手动审查，但大多数时候我得到的正是我想要的。通常，AI 处理某件事的时间越长，其输出就越糟糕，因为这意味着上下文被填满。如果我的 AI 提示执行时间超过 5 分钟，我几乎肯定会停止它并分解任务，或者更具体地说明它应该查看哪些文件或应该首先处理哪些子任务。 让它运行几个小时的人最终绝对不会得到任何值得保留的东西。>通常，人工智能在某件事上工作的时间越长，其输出就越糟糕，因为这意味着上下文被填满。对于旧模型来说，这绝对是正确的，但现在不一定是这样。除了纸上谈兵之外，他们经常做其他事情，这些事情需要花费大量时间，但会得到良好的结果，比如旋转子代理来解决循环中的一些棘手的任务。停止/不信任长时间运行的人工智能是我自己必须忘记的一个习惯。我经常从以下方面获得良好的结果：一次 30 多分钟的 Fable 会话，当我要求它做一些复杂的事情时（例如，循环运行 QA 测试器并消除所有崩溃，每次崩溃修复一次提交）这也是我的经历。 运行一小时的 Fable 会话（一小时的 API 时间）取得了巨大成功。 这里不使用循环，但在构建项目之前，Claude 通常需要先阅读大量文档。对于我来说，长时间运行 Fable 会话的主要失败点是它可能会遇到安全护栏并中途降级到 Opus。确实如此。我发现 5.6 Sol 上限的最佳时间是 6 分钟左右。听起来上面的 OP 查询需要扫描大部分代码库，这本质上会消耗大量输入令牌。 没有地方性。我的工作流程经常在无人值守的情况下运行 8-10 小时。 不过，这是在前沿模型上。但如果我要观察它们，他们会在那段时间提供反馈，但它们不需要转向。非常简单的编码工作流程。 我的大多数操作工作流程要简单得多（因为诊断操作问题显然对时间更加敏感）。作为一个临时 AI 代码用户，我什至无法在 30 秒内克劳德停下来请求第十次运行 bash 脚本的许可。第一步是构建一个沙箱（虚拟机、容器等），让您可以在最宽松的模式下信任代理。这根据我的项目的成熟度而有所不同，但我的大多数会话都位于具有非常特定的出口规则和权限的虚拟机中。令人沮丧的是，没有一个编码工具默认只是启动一个安装了工作目录的 Docker 容器，并让 AI 在容器内疯狂运行。您可以轻松地为自己工作。 我的代理环境现在有 3 种不同类型的计算抽象、3 个不同的代理和一个新生的工作流引擎。它按照我想要的方式工作，这确实令人耳目一新。我认为这是代理工具开发人员应该花费大量精力的领域。 期望用户只是为了安全地使用工具而设置和维护虚拟机是不合理的。 当我运行 cmake 时，我不必担心它会意外删除我的根文件系统或将我的帐户凭据发布到 github。 我使用的大多数命令行编码工具甚至不需要/没有 --dry-run 选项。可能是。 但是a）我不知道为什么我会相信他们会这样做，而现有的工具意味着我不必这么做；b）这对我来说不是一个新范例。 很长一段时间以来，我的大部分开发环境都在沙盒模式下运行。 我也不信任 npm。我想这 8-10 个小时中的大部分时间都花在了调用工具并从中获取反馈上？今天我有两个代理人从晚上 7 点到早上 6 点工作...... 对我来说这是例行公事。今天，我们需要迁移到更便宜的 CI/CD 提供商，并为特定任务制作更好的机器人。 GPT-5.6 溶胶。我遇到了_完全相同_的问题。 从 Kimi.com 获得每月 20 美元的订阅（按年付费），通过 OpenCode + Kimi K2.7 通过他们的 API 进行简单提示，就用完了我的 5 小时配额。 Cursor 在几分钟内完成了相同的提示。根据他们的网络界面，我每周的使用量也达到了 23%。 这感觉很疯狂，因为我在 20 美元 Cursor 计划上做了更多的事情，甚至从未收到警告。我以为 OpenCode 做错了什么，但你对 Kimi Code 也有同样的问题。Moonshot AI 有两种订阅原型，他们从主页链接到的订阅原型是 Web 界面/研究/代理/OpenClaw（他们为您托管）的订阅。这些计划的图块下方有一个小链接，上面写着“希望将其用于代码？ 使用 Kimi 代码”，点击后您将获得一个外观基本相同的订阅页面，其中包含不同的子价格和更多配额非编码计划：* Plus（19“基本代理”——无论是什么）* Pro（39 x2）* Max（99 x6）* Ultra（199 x12）> https://www.kimi.com/membership/pricing编码计划：* Starter（19 -“基本编码”）* Explorer（49 - x4）*专家 (99 x10) * 大师 (219 x25) > https://www.kimi.com/code 我在尝试 Kimi K2.5 发布时偶然发现了这一点。还值得一提的是，20 美元可以让你获得 0.25 的 API 使用优先级 - 因为其他人获得 x4 优先级。 所以准备好享受_缓慢_的体验吧太感谢了。 我正想向您询问链接，因为它们有多个看起来相似的域（kimi.ai、kimi.com、moonshot.ai 等）。 我不确定哪一个是合法的，或者它们是否都是合法的。我得到的计划称为“Moderato”，不知何故，它不是这 8 个中的任何一个...... 我会尝试阅读更多相关内容。现在情况似乎不再如此了。 他们只有一套计划：Allegreto、modereto 等，适用于聊天应用程序和编码应用程序。可以确认的是，我在两个页面上都看到了这些每月价格：Moderato：每年 19 美元或 15 美元Allegretto：每年 39 美元或 31 美元Allegro：每年 99 美元或 79 美元Vivace：每年 199 美元或 159 美元但是，他们说这些计划可能会再次拆分：> 新会员计划即将推出> Kimi 和 Kimi Code 福利将分开。 现有订阅者不受影响。> 您仍然可以在发布前购买当前计划。与推文相同：> 今后，我们还将把会员资格分为两个更有针对性的计划：Kimi 会员资格，适用于 Kimi Web、App 和 Work；Kimi 会员资格适用于 Kimi Web、App 和 Work。和 Kimi Code Membership 用于编码工作流程。 这将有助于我们更精确地匹配计算并保持体验稳定。问题是我不知道是现在就扣动一年一度的Vivace还是等待分拆之后——是否会因为需求而抢钱，或者分拆后购买是否可以省点钱。前几天我和克劳德也有同样的经历。 我向它提出了一些相当简单的问题，但它用完了我整个 5 小时的配额，但没有给出答案。 感觉完全是浪费时间。令我有点惊讶的是，macos web 演示显然是按照每月 40 美元的编码计划完成的，这就像一个 6 小时的任务我认为 Kimi 的东西非常酷，特别是他们有这么多的 RNN/线性注意力层（比他们的完全注意力多 3 倍）。 不过我还没有尝试过。 对于长上下文任务来说，这似乎是非常合理的，我想这符合时代。我怀疑它有这么多参数的原因与计算最优 xLSTM 有很多参数的原因是一样的，这个模型的成功让我有点不高兴，因为我们还没有在欧洲开发出一个大规模的 xLSTM 风格的模型。显然这些人非常务实，除了内部评估之外，他们可能不会致力于任何其他事情，所以他们仍然有普通的注意力层模型等等，我们不能保证提出好的模型的人会以理想的方式进行工程，但我仍然认为 Kimi 的成功表明，如果我们有足够的大型超级计算机用于法学硕士培训，并将它们提供给合适的人，会发生什么——因为这基本上是 Hochreiter 的事情。 它是 RNN，或者说，主要是 RNN。Transformer 架构的初衷之一不就是摆脱 RNN，因为它们无法并行化吗？ 不是专家，几年前只读过几篇论文。是的，尽管后来人们想出了完全或部分并行化 RNN 的合理方法。 还需要在变压器中进行查找。 查询仍然需要与所有旧密钥相乘，因此 RNN 不必与并行性完全不兼容。 只是你不能像线性 RNN h_t = Ah_t + Bx_t 那样在它们内部有一个矩阵，你必须一步一步相乘。 如果
